behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names all four files modified/tested (api.v1.projects.$projectRef.$env.ts, buildImage.ts, CONTRIBUTING.md, supervisor/README.md), describes the API_ORIGIN vs APP_ORIGIN issue, the localhost/Docker issue on macOS, and the incomplete build instructions. The test assertions check for concepts (apiUrl preferring API_ORIGIN, localhost→host.docker.internal on darwin, file structure) all discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md describes the problems (no separate API origin config, localhost unreachable in Docker on macOS, incomplete build docs) but does not specify exact code changes, line numbers, or replacement strings. A developer must reason about what to change.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_normalize_localhost_darwin extracts a function named exactly 'normalizeApiUrlForBuild' via regex match on 'function normalizeApiUrlForBuild'. An alternative correct fix (e.g., inline replacement, a differently-named helper, or a URL transformation done elsewhere) would fail this test. The test also assumes the function body can be extracted with a specific regex pattern and executed standalone via `new Function('apiUrl', body)`, which constrains implementation to a very specific structure.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests extract code and execute it via Node.js subprocess calls (eval expressions, construct functions from extracted bodies, run them with mock data). The pass-to-pass tests also execute Node.js to check syntax/structure. They go beyond simple text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_api_origin_preferred_over_app_origin evaluates the actual apiUrl expression with mock env variables and checks specific output values. test_normalize_localhost_darwin executes the extracted function and checks that localhost is replaced with host.docker.internal. A stub/no-op would not produce these outputs.

behavior_in_tests.reason: instruction.md explicitly requires CONTRIBUTING.md updates (build instructions) and supervisor/README.md changes, but no test validates these documentation changes. The test file has a commented-out placeholder '# [config_edit] fail_to_pass' with no actual test function for CONTRIBUTING.md or supervisor/README.md edits.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are organized with clear section comments (fail-to-pass pr_diff, fail-to-pass config_edit, pass-to-pass static, pass-to-pass repo_tests), each function has a descriptive name and docstring, and tags like [pr_diff] and [static] are used.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves code/config edits, not structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB schemas.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, and commands in instruction.md and tests are consistent and correctly spelled.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names files relevant to the problem domain (route endpoint, build logic, docs) but doesn't specify exact line numbers or exact code to write. The file mentions are appropriate for guiding the developer to the right area.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold config/doc edits (CONTRIBUTING.md line 71/73, supervisor/README.md line 39) are localized single-line changes, not full-file rewrites, and don't contradict other files.
